color_legend.green: Keep: upstream engines confirm good enough language and useful IELTS function.
color_legend.yellow: Improve/check: upstream engines found repair need or sentence-control confirmation is missing.
color_legend.red: Rewrite: upstream engines show low control, multiple local problems, or paragraph-function pressure.

revision_waves.title: Wave 1 — Confirm language-control signals
revision_waves.text: 1 sentence(s) do not have evaluator sentence-control confirmation, so they are not treated as Keep.

revision_waves.title: Wave 3 — Improve/check yellow areas
revision_waves.text: Paragraph(s) 1, 2, 3, 4, 5: check language and paragraph function before keeping these sentences.

revision_waves.title: Wave 4 — Final language pass

revision_waves.student_text: After rewriting, check only changed sentences for grammar, vocabulary, spelling, and paragraph flow.

copyable_clean_text:
Community service has become an increasingly common topic in discussions about high school education. While some argue that it should remain a voluntary choice, I largely agree that making it compulsory would bring more benefits than drawbacks, provided it is implemented thoughtfully.

One of the main advantages of compulsory community service is that it exposes students to realities they might otherwise never encounter. Many teenagers grow up in fairly protected environments and rarely interact with people from different backgrounds, such as elderly residents in care homes or families experiencing poverty. By requiring students to volunteer, schools can help young people develop empathy and a broader understanding of society, which is something that classroom lessons alone often fail to achieve.

In addition, community service can teach practical skills that are not always covered in the standard curriculum, including teamwork, communication, and time management. These skills are valuable not only for future employment but also for personal growth. Universities and employers increasingly value applicants who can demonstrate real-world experience alongside academic achievement.

On the other hand, critics point out that students already face heavy academic pressure, and adding mandatory service hours could increase stress rather than reduce it. This is a fair concern, but it can be addressed by allowing flexible scheduling, such as completing hours during holidays or weekends, rather than during term time.

Overall, although there are some practical challenges to consider, I believe the long-term benefits of compulsory community service, particularly in building empathy and life skills, outweigh the potential downsides.
revision_editor_seed:
Community service has become an increasingly common topic in discussions about high school education. While some argue that it should remain a voluntary choice, I largely agree that making it compulsory would bring more benefits than drawbacks, provided it is implemented thoughtfully.

One of the main advantages of compulsory community service is that it exposes students to realities they might otherwise never encounter. Many teenagers grow up in fairly protected environments and rarely interact with people from different backgrounds, such as elderly residents in care homes or families experiencing poverty. By requiring students to volunteer, schools can help young people develop empathy and a broader understanding of society, which is something that classroom lessons alone often fail to achieve.

In addition, community service can teach practical skills that are not always covered in the standard curriculum, including teamwork, communication, and time management. These skills are valuable not only for future employment but also for personal growth. Universities and employers increasingly value applicants who can demonstrate real-world experience alongside academic achievement.

On the other hand, critics point out that students already face heavy academic pressure, and adding mandatory service hours could increase stress rather than reduce it. This is a fair concern, but it can be addressed by allowing flexible scheduling, such as completing hours during holidays or weekends, rather than during term time.

Overall, although there are some practical challenges to consider, I believe the long-term benefits of compulsory community service, particularly in building empathy and life skills, outweigh the potential downsides.

student_checklist: I checked every yellow sentence before keeping it.
I did not treat a sentence as green unless the app marked it green.
I checked the introduction: no example, only task framing, position, and main arguments.
I checked the conclusion: final answer and main reasons, no new example.
I checked spelling and grammar after rewriting.

pre_revision_correction_policy.reason: The learner should self-correct before seeing a model version.

errormap_rows_used.sentence: While some argue that it should remain a voluntary choice, I largely agree that making it compulsory would bring more benefits than drawbacks, provided it is implemented thoughtfully.

Overall, although there are some practical challenges to consider, I believe the long-term benefits of compulsory community service, particularly in building empathy and life skills, outweigh the potential downsides.

prewriting_guidance.title: Before you write or revise

structure_options.name: Universal IELTS Task 2 structure
paragraphs: Introduction: task frame, clear answer/position, preview main ideas.
Body 1: one main idea, explanation, specific example, link.
Body 2: one main idea, explanation, specific example, link.
Conclusion: summary + final answer. No new example or idea.

body_paragraph_formula: Topic sentence: say the paragraph's main idea.
Argument/reason: explain why the idea matters.
Development: add cause, effect, comparison, or consequence.
Link back: connect the example to the question or your position.

example_quality.strong_example_rule: Use a specific place/actor/programme/situation, show what happened, and link it to the argument.
example_quality.weak_example_rule: Avoid private family anecdotes or very general examples as the main evidence.
